prevalence.trend: increasing due to urbanization and dietary shifts

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on ISG guidelines: upper GI endoscopy for confirmation, symptom-based (heartburn, regurgitation) using Rome IV criteria adapted for Indian context

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low (~10-15%) due to limited endoscopy access in rural areas

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ISG-endorsed: endoscopy preferred, non-invasive urea breath test for H. pylori in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: PPIs (e.g., pantoprazole) as first-line, triple therapy for H. pylori eradication, Ayurvedic options like Avipattikar churna for symptom relief
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: high H. pylori prevalence (>70%), spicy diet culture exacerbating symptoms, limited endoscopy access in rural India
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: generic PPI availability at low cost, integration of Ayurveda with allopathic care in public health programs
